candidates.stmt:
Thank you for reading my election pitch.

It’s a privilege to have the opportunity to represent Balderstone & Kirkholt, as your Labour & Co-operative candidate.

I believe voters should be informed and able to make the right decision when choosing who to represent you and your family’s needs. You want someone who will work tirelessly to make Balderstone and Kirkholt a better place for us all to live. Someone who already has a proven track record of working in the community and who knows that actions speak louder than words.

It’s worth knowing that the person you put your trust in will deliver the best outcome for you. I will do everything I can to make a difference and would be proud to represent you on Rochdale Council.

I was born in Rochdale, and grew up here. I live and work here with my wife and two young children. I want Rochdale to be a great place to grow up in, and a great place to grow old in. I am a volunteer with Rochdale in Bloom and enjoy earning our town the national recognition it deserves.

As your Labour candidate I have already been working with your Labour team to resolve local issues and to improve facilities in our area. I’ve been working to make a difference by:

Getting our footpaths cleaned and brought back into use
Working with the police to tackle speeding vehicles
Having illegal fly-tipping and litter removed from the park and our public areas
Working with local community and voluntary groups to provide more activities for younger and older residents
Taking up your concerns with landlords such as RBH
Having potholes filled, and roads repaired.

Your experienced Labour team have supported many residents in the last year, but it’s you and your family that have made the real sacrifices and changes to your daily lives to keep friends and neighbours safe. Our NHS and frontline workers have been heroic but have struggled to deal with Coronavirus due to years of Conservative cuts in our public services and wasteful outsourcin

Married with 5 children and with experiance in law. I am willing to stand up for my community.

If like me you want to see Balderstone and Kirkholt improved and changes made that benefit residents.

Then I ask you to join me in my campaign to make Balderstone and Kirkholt proud again.

Our Ward is demanding change and innovation.

Previously served as a Conservative Councillor for St Mary's Ward in Trafford from 2010 to 2022. Mayor of Trafford 2019 to 2020.

Last stood in Trafford successfully in 2018: https://whocanivotefor.co.uk/person/41320/rob-chilton

I have lived here many years and people in the area know me as hard working reliable and dedicated to my family and my community.
I am committed to serving the community and representing local voices. Let us build a brighter future together! As a party we are dedicated to our community not to politics, “PEOPLE before Politics” is our motto.
I am a family man with grown up children. With a long-suffering wife who is the love of my life. 
Bamford needs help and support I am dedicated to not only giving that support. But to holding the Labour Party’s feet to the fire over its neglect of the Town. My friends and family have been urging me to stand for some time.

I am proud to call Central Ward in Rochdale my home. I am a local lad, born and raised in the very community I now seek to represent. Rochdale is not just where I live — it is where I grew up, built my life, and where my children are growing up today.
I work as a retail manager, bringing years of leadership, organisation, and frontline experience in supporting people and managing teams. I understand the challenges working families face because I live them every day. I am married and a proud father of four children — three daughters and one son — and like many parents in our ward, I want a safe, thriving, and opportunity-rich future for them.

My name is Paul Beswick and I am proud to announce that I intend to stand in the Local council elections on May 5th 2022 as a member of the Middleton Independent Party for East Middleton Ward.

I have lived in Middleton since I married 46 years ago. Throughout my life in Middleton I have watched this town I moved to be run down to a shadow of its former status.

This has been done by the Party Politics of the main Parties. We seem to be an afterthought to what Rochdale town thinks it should have.

Our Arena was only built because Rochdale Council made a deal with Tesco and other parties, at what cost to the make up of our town.

Now it’s about time Middleton peoples voices were heard, not through the mainstream parties, but your own Party, Middleton Independents Party.

I have joined the Middleton Independents to make sure Middleton's voice is heard in the Council Chamber and to fight for a better town, so with your three votes on May 5th, (don’t forget you have 3 votes this year), vote for the Middleton people that will act for Middleton, not some mainstream Party candidate who has to toe the party line.

I am, if elected, here to listen to your voices and act upon your concerns to the best of my ability, I may not be too politically savvy of the mainstream politics, but NO voice from Middleton means no voice being heard in Rochdale Town Hall to fight for our town.

Regards

Paul Beswick

A local voice for Healey, Shawclough and Syke

I’m standing to be your councillor because I believe our area deserves someone who will listen, act, and stand up for residents.

I was born in Rochdale, raised in Deeplish, and have lived and worked locally for most of my life. This isn’t just where I’m standing — it’s my home.

Over the past few years, I’ve been actively working in the community, raising issues that matter to residents, including:

Ongoing planning breaches affecting Shawclough residents
Road safety concerns near local schools
Dangerous footpaths and poor road conditions
The need for better communication and accountability from the Council

Too often, residents tell me they feel ignored or that nothing gets done. That needs to change.

If elected, my priorities are simple:

Fix the issues that affect everyday life in our area
Ensure residents’ concerns are properly heard and acted upon
Push for real accountability from the Council
Work with the community to deliver practical solutions

This campaign isn’t about politics for the sake of it — it’s about getting things done for our community.

At the last election, we came within 131 votes of second place. This time, with your support, we can go further.

If you want a councillor who is visible, approachable, and willing to fight for local residents, I would be honoured to have your vote.

Andy Lord
Liberal Democrat Candidate for Healey Ward

Time for better in Healey, Shawclough and Syke

It’s an honour to stand as your Green Party candidate for Healey in this election.

I’m a Rochdalian, a homeowner in the ward, a father of three, and a teacher who has supported local young people for over 20 years. I care deeply about this community, as it has done so much for me and my family — and I know how much more it could be.

Because right now, too many people feel let down — after years of austerity, poor decision-making, and divisive politics that put parties before people.

For years, we’ve seen the same problems — roads not fixed, antisocial behaviour brushed aside, rising costs hitting families hard, and decisions made without properly listening to residents.

The local council has had years to get this right. Others shout loudly but offer little that improves daily life.

You deserve better.

I’m standing to offer something different: honest, practical leadership that puts residents first.

If elected, I will:

1. Be a visible, accessible councillor who listens and acts
2. Tackle antisocial behaviour and make our streets safer
3. Fix roads, traffic and parking problems properly
4. Support families through the cost of living crisis
5. Protect green spaces and take action on the Turner Brothers site
6. Bring real transparency and accountability to the council

No empty promises. No excuses. No division. Just action — as one ward.

Across Greater Manchester, the Green Party is winning — in places like Gorton &  Denton, and local by-elections — because people want something better.

We are a breath of fresh air — and a real alternative.

On May 7th, send a clear message.

Vote Green.

Umar Ali
Green Party candidate for Healey Ward

It’s an honour to once again be selected as candidate for my home ward of Healey in these local elections.

Whereas some candidates in our ward have little if any connection to our area, I’ve lived here for over ten years and have spent that time serving local residents on a range of issues from financial issues, fly-tipping, planning and roads.  I have also served as a school governor in the ward.

This year we have some parties looking to make this election about propaganda and political point scoring, instead of serving residents.  I’m standing because this is my home, my children live here in the ward and attend local schools, we live, work and play here - like you and what happens here impacts us all directly.

I believe in hard work, responsibility and fairness and in that light, this is what you’ll get from me as your new councillor!

1. The most accessible representative you’ve ever had!
2. A voice for you, not just a lost voice in a majority party!
3. Action to protect our green spaces and demand infrastructure before development!
4. Action to keep council tax down!
5. A zero tolerance approach to anti social behaviour!
6. Support real regeneration!
7. Fix roads and infrastructure as a priority!
8. Bring fairness, transparency and respect to local government!

On May 7th, don’t get caught up in the hype, propaganda and lies - vote for action Vote Rob!

Hi, My name is Steve Potter and will be standing for The Reform Party in the constituency Heywood & Middleton North. I have lived in Heywood for 30 years and watched its decline. I am standing to change this.

I will stop the new proposed transport plans that will potentially close businesses and create huge traffic chaos. Many local issues are driven by national policy, mass immigration and the cost of our obsessive net zero at all cost ideology.

For far too long MP's and Councillors over the years have not done anything to improve our constituency and forgotten who they work for. I will work for and on behalf of the people  of Heywood and Middleton North to improve our constituency. The residents of Heywood and Middleton North deserve better.

I'm standing for Reform UK because I have had enough of the mediocre representation we have received over the last few decades that has led us to where we are today.

If you have had enough, get involved, join us, volunteer and help us campaign.

If you would like to donate to my campaign to cover costs for leaflets / advertising etc you can do so by clicking donate button below. If you have any questions or would like to know more pop me an email. Let's Make Britain Great Again!!

Hello, I’m Robert King, and I am standing for the Green Party of England and Wales as your local councillor in the ward of Hopwood Hall.

Growing up and working in this area has shown me how deeply I care about the future of our community. Like so many of us, I want to see a council that listens to its residents, invests in our neighbourhoods, and puts people before politics.

I am standing because I truly believe that local people deserve a stronger voice and better representation on our council.

If elected, I will focus on:

– Protecting and improving our local green spaces and environment.

– Standing up for safer, cleaner streets and stronger local services.

– Making sure ordinary people in Hopwood Hall have a real say in the decisions that affect their lives.

I wholeheartedly believe that a council should be open, accountable, and accessible. This is why I will hold regular surgeries for residents to speak directly to me about the issues that matter to them.

On 7 May 2026, I ask for your vote so that Hopwood Hall can have a better future for all of us.

Thank you.

Iain Donaldson is proud to stand as the Liberal Democrat candidate for Hopwood Hall ward. He is committed to listening to residents and delivering practical solutions on the issues that matter most, from filling potholes and creating safer streets and reliable local services to protecting tackling polution, protecting green spaces and supporting community wellbeing. Iain believes in openness, fairness, and giving people a stronger voice in local decisions. With a focus on accountability and hard work, he aims to bring positive change to the area and ensure Hopwood Hall remains a great place to live, work, and raise a family for everyone in the community.

Together let’s make things happen. I am happily married, young dynamic and up for putting Rochdale  back on the map. The school system let me down. I am determined to ensure this does not happen to a single child in Rochdale ever again. I was lucky, I work in my family confectionary business. I support my local community and want the best for them. During my working life I have been lucky enough to develop a range of skills. But I see the young people in my ward cannot get help with skills and learning experiences because we have no youth centres.

Link for Rochdale Green Party’s manifesto

https://rochdale.greenparty.org.uk/our-local-manifesto/

Hello, my name is Mark Hollinrake, and I am standing to be your local councillor for Kingsway ward. I am a father of 4 and grandparent of 6, and lived on Turf Hill for 32 years, so I know about the various problems that need tackling in the ward and the Borough of Rochdale.

I am a proud Eco Socialist,  and ex Unite and PCS shop steward and I believe that the Green Party can bring Real hope and Real Positive Change in Kingsway and the Borough of Rochdale.

If elected, I will focus on:

– Protecting and improving our local green spaces and environment.

– Standing up for safer, cleaner streets and stronger local services.

– Making sure ordinary people in Kingsway have a real say in the decisions that affect their lives.

I believe in an open, accessible Council and will hold regular open surgeries throughout the ward to listen to the views and problems of residents.

I ask for the chance to represent you , so please consider voting for me on Thursday 7th May 2026, to help build a better Kingsway and Borough of Rochdale

Liberal Democrat Party
South Middleton Ward

Address
31 Bay Street
Rochdale
OL12 0EA

I am proud to have been selected to represent South Middleton at the local elections on 6th May for Rochdale Liberal Democrats. I am born and bred in Rochdale, I am married and I work as an accountant for a company in Oldham. My parents also live in Rochdale.

I am passionate about the borough and making it a better place to live, work, rest and play.

South Middleton has its own unique identity and a strong community spirit. If you elect me, I will be your local hard working Councillor serving all our residents in our ward and speaking up for our area and getting our fair share of funding and services at the town hall.

In these local elections, I will be campaigning for:

A clean, green and safe environment
Roads and pavements free from potholes
Campaigning against development on our Greenfield’s
Safer roads
More services for our young people
Improved transport services
If you elect me on 6th May, I will work tirelessly to represent you in the town hall and in our community.

Email: Emma4SouthMiddleton@gmail.com

Dear Resident,

I was born and bred in Milkstone and Deeplish. These streets shaped me. The schools, the neighbours, the local shops, the mosques and churches, the parks — this isn’t just a ward to me. It’s home.

I know the pride we have in our community. I also know the pressures families are under right now.

The cost of living crisis isn’t a headline here — it’s real. It’s parents worrying about bills, pensioners choosing between heating and food, small businesses struggling to stay afloat. I see it. I hear it. And I will fight every day to make sure our council does more to support local families and puts our ward first when decisions are made.

I’ve also seen the health inequalities that affect our community. Too many of our residents experience poorer health outcomes simply because of where we live. That is not acceptable. I will push for better access to GPs, mental health services, and preventative health support so Milkstone and Deeplish is no longer left behind.

We deserve cleaner streets and neighbourhoods we can feel proud of. Fly-tipping, litter, and neglected spaces are not a reflection of who we are — they’re a reflection of weak enforcement and poor priorities. I will demand tougher action and real accountability.

And when it comes to roadworks and disruption, residents deserve proper communication and planning — not chaos that hurts local businesses and frustrates families trying to get on with their day.

Beyond local issues, I have always believed that standing up for justice matters. My advocacy for the people of Gaza comes from a place of conscience and humanity. I believe our representatives should have the courage to speak up for human rights and fairness — at home and abroad.

I’m not an outsider looking in. I’m one of you. I understand this community because it raised me. I want to give back through honest, visible and principled representation.

Milkstone and Deeplish deserves someone who will work hard, listen closely, and never forget

Born and bred in Rochdale, I’ve seen our town decline while wasteful spending continues. Milnrow and Newhey deserve better. As your Reform UK candidate, I’ll challenge financial waste, fix roads and traffic issues, and stand up for residents who feel ignored. Your voice matters — elect me and make it heard.
Classical liberal. Co-operative values. Enlightenment principles. Quaker ethics.

Dear Residents,

For those who may not know me, my name is Keeley O’Mara. I’m proud to announce that I am standing for the Middleton Independents Party, seeking re-election to represent you once again.

A little about me: I grew up in Boarshaw and attended school locally before following a family passion for agriculture by studying at Myerscough College.

Like many residents in Middleton, I initially had little interest in politics or council matters. That changed with the proposed development on the fields on Hollin Lane (Taylor Wimpey). The lack of support from councillors, despite over 1,000 objections, made me question the system. I realised that simply voicing concerns on social media wasn’t enough—I wanted to be where decisions were being made.

This led me to stand as an independent candidate in 2017. I came a strong second to Labour, which both surprised and encouraged me. It showed that Middleton residents were ready for change—but the right kind of change.

Following that election, I was approached by the Conservatives, Liberal Democrats, and UKIP. However, joining a national party didn’t feel right for me or align with my values. Instead, I chose to focus on serving our community directly.

I became involved in a number of local initiatives. I was part of the early development of Middleton in Bloom and attended its first meeting after raising ideas about improvements such as railway signage in Heywood. I remained involved for several years. During Covid, I supported Middleton in Action, helping deliver food parcels, prescriptions, and vital support. I also volunteer with Middleton Roundtable at events including MiddFest, Remembrance Sunday, and the Christmas lights switch-on.

The past two years have been incredibly challenging. I lost my dad in August 2024, which was devastating. The by-election was announced on the day of his funeral, and I wasn’t sure I had the strength to campaign. However, with the support of my party and local residents, I stood—and

I am a personal & career development coach as well as a Qualified Teacher, motivational speaker, the founder and CEO of Harvest Recruitment Group and Harvest Touch Youth & skills community Hub based in Rochdale Manchester United Kingdom. as well as an immigration consultant. and a mother of two beautiful girls. I am passionate about helping people unlock their full potential, both personally and professionally. 
I am young dynamic and full of ideas, which is the direct opposite of this tired run-down council. Over the years, I have had the privilege of guiding individuals through career transitions, building their confidence, and helping them achieve success in the workplace. 
Recently, I launched my book, Yes! I Got the Job, which is a practical and empowering guide for anyone navigating the challenges of the job market, from interviews to landing their dream role. 
I am really excited to be here, to share make an impact by bringing my blessing on you all as your perspective candidate to make a change in Spotland & Falinge.

Dear Resident,
I hope you will consider supporting me in the
forthcoming local elections. It would be a pleasure and an honour to represent the Wardle, Shore and West Littleborough ward and with your support I would like to be your elected representative and your voice within the council. 
As your ward councillor I would listen very
carefully to the issues that are of the utmost importance to you and I share your concerns.

Let me tell you a little bit about myself;
I am a 68-year-old musician with a passion for community service who with your help would
very much like be your next locally elected champion for our ward. I was born and bred here, this is where my heart lies and this is where I want to use my experience and local
knowledge to improve the area we call home.
I have a compassionate spirit with the interests of others at heart and co-founded the Healey Dell Heritage Centre and Tea Rooms in 2013, a volunteer-driven initiative supporting vulnerable people with learning difficulties and rehabilitation needs. An incredible project and one that supported many Individuals helping them reach their full potential. My desire to help others goes much further too, I was a trustee and director of The Heroes Project (Forever Manchester), supporting young adults with disabilities and was honoured as Mensa Businessman of the Year in 1996.

I have been fortunate and successful in life and now want to use my experience and ability
to further the interests of our ward. It has been very clear from speaking to many residents over recent weeks how we share a strong sense of local priorities. In other words, your priorities are my priorities too;
• The constant over development of our area with an aging infrastructure that simply
cannot cope with the additional demands.
• Basic council services such as bin collections and highway maintenance are failing and yet our council tax increases at an alarming rate.
• The cost-of-living crisis is a financial worry for us all and yet ou

I am Matthew Hargreaves, your Conservative candidate for Wardle, West Littleborough & Shore, and a proud member of this community.

For over four decades, the Conservative Group, led by Ashley Dearnley, has worked tirelessly to serve our area. Together, they have defended our precious green spaces, brought utility companies and Highways authorities together to improve our road infrastructure, and pushed the Council to deliver fairer outcomes, including council tax rebates for residents.

Most importantly, they have always stood up for the people they represent.

I am standing for election to continue that strong record of service. My commitment is simple: to work hard, listen to residents, and ensure our community’s voice is heard loud and clear at every level of decision-making.

As someone who lives here, I understand the issues that matter most to you. I will build on the work already done and continue striving to make Wardle, West Littleborough & Shore an even better place to live.

I ask for your support so I can represent you and our community with dedication, integrity, and determination.

1. Traffic lights at Argyle / Pilsworth junction with pedestrian crossings
2. Shop Lifting deterrent; businesses having access to a police monitored communication system and Shop Safety group.
3. Tram to Heywood and better bus connectivity
4.Ensuring that no new premises in Heywood centre become takeaways
5. Investment in Heywood centre and business support to encourage new businesses to start up
6. Even more youth provision to give our young people opportunities to thrive
7. Ensuring the Heywood £20million over 10 years funding is spent in a way driven by the community and used to draw in additional money
